# Bun

Bun is an all-in-one toolkit for JavaScript and TypeScript applications that ships as a single executable. At its core is the Bun runtime, a fast JavaScript runtime designed as a drop-in replacement for Node.js, written in Zig and powered by JavaScriptCore. Bun dramatically reduces startup times and memory usage compared to Node.js, handling roughly 2.5x more HTTP requests per second.

Beyond the runtime, Bun includes a native bundler, a Jest-compatible test runner, and a Node.js-compatible package manager that's up to 25x faster than npm. TypeScript and JSX are supported out-of-the-box with zero configuration. Bun implements Web APIs like `fetch`, `WebSocket`, and `Response`, along with Node.js APIs for drop-in compatibility with existing projects.

## Bun.serve - HTTP Server

Create high-performance HTTP servers with built-in routing, WebSocket support, and automatic request handling. Supports static routes, dynamic routes with parameters, and per-HTTP method handlers.

```typescript
const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,
  routes: {
    // Static response
    "/api/status": new Response("OK"),

    // Dynamic route with parameters
    "/users/:id": (req) => {
      return Response.json({ userId: req.params.id });
    },

    // Per-method handlers
    "/api/posts": {
      GET: () => Response.json({ posts: [] }),
      POST: async (req) => {
        const body = await req.json();
        return Response.json({ created: true, ...body }, { status: 201 });
      },
    },

    // Wildcard route
    "/api/*": Response.json({ message: "Not found" }, { status: 404 }),

    // Serve static file
    "/favicon.ico": Bun.file("./favicon.ico"),
  },

  // Fallback for unmatched routes
  fetch(req) {
    return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  },

  // Error handler
  error(error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  },
});

console.log(`Server running at ${server.url}`);
// Server running at http://localhost:3000

// Hot reload routes without restart
server.reload({
  routes: {
    "/api/version": () => Response.json({ version: "2.0.0" }),
  },
});

// Graceful shutdown
await server.stop();
```

## Bun.serve WebSocket

Server-side WebSocket implementation with pub/sub, compression, and contextual data per connection. 7x more throughput than Node.js + ws.

```typescript
type WebSocketData = {
  username: string;
  joinedAt: number;
};

const server = Bun.serve({
  fetch(req, server) {
    const url = new URL(req.url);
    if (url.pathname === "/chat") {
      const username = url.searchParams.get("user") || "anonymous";
      const success = server.upgrade(req, {
        data: { username, joinedAt: Date.now() },
        headers: { "Set-Cookie": "session=abc123" },
      });
      return success ? undefined : new Response("Upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
    }
    return new Response("Hello");
  },

  websocket: {
    // Type ws.data across all handlers
    data: {} as WebSocketData,

    open(ws) {
      ws.subscribe("chat-room");
      server.publish("chat-room", `${ws.data.username} joined`);
    },

    message(ws, message) {
      // Broadcast to all subscribers
      server.publish("chat-room", `${ws.data.username}: ${message}`);
    },

    close(ws) {
      ws.unsubscribe("chat-room");
      server.publish("chat-room", `${ws.data.username} left`);
    },

    // Configuration
    idleTimeout: 120,          // seconds
    maxPayloadLength: 1024 * 1024, // 1 MB
    perMessageDeflate: true,   // compression
  },
});

// Publish from server to all subscribers
server.publish("chat-room", "Server announcement!");
```

## Bun.file and Bun.write - File I/O

Optimized file operations using the fastest system calls per platform. BunFile is a lazy-loaded file reference conforming to the Blob interface.

```typescript
// Reading files - lazy loaded, doesn't read until needed
const file = Bun.file("data.txt");
console.log(file.size);  // bytes
console.log(file.type);  // MIME type

// Multiple read formats
const text = await file.text();           // string
const json = await file.json();           // parsed JSON
const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer();  // ArrayBuffer
const bytes = await file.bytes();         // Uint8Array
const stream = file.stream();             // ReadableStream

// Check existence
const exists = await file.exists();  // boolean

// Delete file
await Bun.file("logs.json").delete();

// Writing files - returns bytes written
await Bun.write("output.txt", "Hello World");
await Bun.write("data.json", JSON.stringify({ key: "value" }));

// Copy file using optimized system calls
const input = Bun.file("source.txt");
await Bun.write("destination.txt", input);

// Write HTTP response body to file
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
await Bun.write("page.html", response);

// Write to stdout
await Bun.write(Bun.stdout, "Hello stdout\n");

// Incremental writing with FileSink
const writer = Bun.file("log.txt").writer({ highWaterMark: 1024 * 1024 });
writer.write("Line 1\n");
writer.write("Line 2\n");
writer.flush();  // write buffer to disk
writer.end();    // flush and close
```

## bun:sqlite - SQLite Database

High-performance synchronous SQLite driver, 3-6x faster than better-sqlite3. Features transactions, prepared statements, and class mapping.

```typescript
import { Database } from "bun:sqlite";

// Create/open database
const db = new Database("mydb.sqlite");
const memoryDb = new Database(":memory:");
const readonlyDb = new Database("mydb.sqlite", { readonly: true });

// Enable strict mode for better error handling
const strictDb = new Database(":memory:", { strict: true });

// Enable WAL mode for better performance
db.run("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;");

// Create table
db.run(`
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT UNIQUE,
    created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  )
`);

// Prepared statements (cached and reusable)
const insertUser = db.query("INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ($name, $email)");
const result = insertUser.run({ $name: "Alice", $email: "alice@example.com" });
console.log(result.lastInsertRowid);  // 1
console.log(result.changes);          // 1

// Query methods
const allUsers = db.query("SELECT * FROM users").all();          // array of objects
const oneUser = db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?").get(1);  // single object or undefined
const values = db.query("SELECT name, email FROM users").values(); // array of arrays

// Iterate large result sets
for (const row of db.query("SELECT * FROM users").iterate()) {
  console.log(row.name);
}

// Map results to class instances
class User {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  get displayName() {
    return `User: ${this.name}`;
  }
}
const users = db.query("SELECT * FROM users").as(User).all();
console.log(users[0].displayName);  // "User: Alice"

// Transactions (atomic, auto-rollback on error)
const insertMany = db.transaction((users) => {
  for (const user of users) {
    insertUser.run(user);
  }
  return users.length;
});

const count = insertMany([
  { $name: "Bob", $email: "bob@example.com" },
  { $name: "Carol", $email: "carol@example.com" },
]);
console.log(`Inserted ${count} users`);

// Transaction modes
insertMany.deferred(users);   // BEGIN DEFERRED
insertMany.immediate(users);  // BEGIN IMMEDIATE
insertMany.exclusive(users);  // BEGIN EXCLUSIVE

// Close database
db.close();
```

## Bun.build - Bundler

Fast native bundler for JavaScript, TypeScript, and JSX with tree-shaking, code splitting, and multiple output formats.

```typescript
// Basic bundling
const result = await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./src/index.tsx"],
  outdir: "./dist",
});

if (!result.success) {
  console.error("Build failed:", result.logs);
}

// Full configuration
await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./src/index.tsx", "./src/worker.ts"],
  outdir: "./dist",
  target: "browser",     // "browser" | "bun" | "node"
  format: "esm",         // "esm" | "cjs" | "iife"
  splitting: true,       // enable code splitting
  minify: true,          // or { whitespace: true, syntax: true, identifiers: true }
  sourcemap: "linked",   // "none" | "linked" | "inline" | "external"

  // External packages (not bundled)
  external: ["react", "react-dom"],

  // Or mark all packages external
  packages: "external",

  // Environment variable handling
  env: "inline",         // or "PUBLIC_*" for prefix matching

  // Define compile-time constants
  define: {
    "process.env.NODE_ENV": JSON.stringify("production"),
  },

  // Custom file loaders
  loader: {
    ".png": "dataurl",
    ".txt": "file",
  },

  // Output naming with tokens
  naming: {
    entry: "[dir]/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
    chunk: "chunks/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
    asset: "assets/[name]-[hash].[ext]",
  },

  // JSX configuration
  jsx: {
    runtime: "automatic",
    importSource: "react",
  },

  // Banner/footer for output
  banner: '"use client";',
  footer: "// Built with Bun",

  // Remove console/debugger
  drop: ["console", "debugger"],

  // Generate build metadata
  metafile: true,
});

// Access build outputs
for (const output of result.outputs) {
  console.log(output.path);    // absolute path
  console.log(output.kind);    // "entry-point" | "chunk" | "asset" | "sourcemap"
  const content = await output.text();  // file contents
}

// CLI equivalent
// bun build ./src/index.tsx --outdir ./dist --minify --splitting --sourcemap=linked
```

## $ Shell - Cross-Platform Shell Scripting

Bash-like shell with JavaScript interop, automatic escaping, and cross-platform support for Windows, Linux, and macOS.

```typescript
import { $ } from "bun";

// Basic commands
await $`echo "Hello World!"`;  // prints to stdout

// Capture output
const result = await $`echo "Hello"`.text();  // "Hello\n"
const json = await $`echo '{"a":1}'`.json();  // { a: 1 }
const lines = await $`cat file.txt`.lines();  // async iterator

// Quiet mode (no stdout)
await $`npm install`.quiet();

// Error handling
try {
  await $`command-that-fails`.text();
} catch (err) {
  console.log(err.exitCode);
  console.log(err.stderr.toString());
}

// Disable throwing on non-zero exit
const { exitCode, stdout } = await $`maybe-fails`.nothrow().quiet();
if (exitCode !== 0) {
  console.log("Command failed");
}

// Piping
await $`cat file.txt | grep "pattern" | wc -l`;

// Redirection
await $`echo "data" > output.txt`;       // stdout to file
await $`cat < input.txt`;                // file to stdin
await $`command 2> errors.txt`;          // stderr to file
await $`command &> all.txt`;             // both to file

// JavaScript interop - use Response, Buffer, etc.
const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
await $`cat < ${response} | wc -c`;

const buffer = Buffer.alloc(100);
await $`echo "Hello" > ${buffer}`;

// Environment variables
await $`FOO=bar bun -e 'console.log(process.env.FOO)'`;
$.env({ NODE_ENV: "production" });  // set globally
await $`echo $NODE_ENV`.env({ NODE_ENV: "test" });  // override locally

// Working directory
await $`pwd`.cwd("/tmp");  // /tmp
$.cwd("/app");             // set globally

// Safe interpolation (auto-escaped)
const userInput = "file.txt; rm -rf /";
await $`ls ${userInput}`;  // treats as single argument, safe!

// Builtin commands (cross-platform)
await $`ls -la`;
await $`rm -rf ./temp`;
await $`mkdir -p ./nested/dir`;
await $`cat file.txt`;
await $`pwd`;
```

## bun:test - Test Runner

Jest-compatible test runner with async support, mocking, snapshots, and code coverage. Run with `bun test`.

```typescript
import { test, expect, describe, beforeEach, afterEach, mock, spyOn } from "bun:test";

// Basic test
test("addition works", () => {
  expect(2 + 2).toBe(4);
});

// Async test
test("fetch works", async () => {
  const response = await fetch("https://example.com");
  expect(response.ok).toBe(true);
});

// Test groups
describe("User API", () => {
  let db;

  beforeEach(() => {
    db = createTestDb();
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    db.close();
  });

  test("creates user", async () => {
    const user = await db.createUser({ name: "Alice" });
    expect(user.id).toBeDefined();
    expect(user.name).toBe("Alice");
  });

  test("validates email", () => {
    expect(() => db.createUser({ email: "invalid" })).toThrow("Invalid email");
  });
});

// Test modifiers
test.skip("not ready yet", () => {});
test.todo("implement later", () => {});
test.only("run only this", () => {});  // use with `bun test --only`

// Conditional tests
test.if(process.platform === "darwin")("macOS only", () => {});
test.skipIf(process.platform === "win32")("skip on Windows", () => {});

// Timeout (default 5000ms)
test("slow operation", async () => {
  await longRunningTask();
}, 10000);

// Retry flaky tests
test("network call", async () => {
  const res = await fetch("https://api.example.com");
  expect(res.ok).toBe(true);
}, { retry: 3 });

// Parametrized tests
test.each([
  [1, 2, 3],
  [2, 3, 5],
  [5, 5, 10],
])("add(%i, %i) = %i", (a, b, expected) => {
  expect(a + b).toBe(expected);
});

// Mocking
const mockFn = mock((x) => x * 2);
mockFn(5);
expect(mockFn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(5);
expect(mockFn).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
expect(mockFn.mock.results[0].value).toBe(10);

// Spy on methods
const obj = { method: (x) => x + 1 };
const spy = spyOn(obj, "method");
obj.method(5);
expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled();

// Matchers
expect(value).toBe(exact);
expect(value).toEqual(deepEqual);
expect(value).toBeTruthy();
expect(value).toBeFalsy();
expect(value).toBeNull();
expect(value).toBeDefined();
expect(array).toContain(item);
expect(array).toHaveLength(3);
expect(string).toMatch(/pattern/);
expect(obj).toHaveProperty("key", "value");
expect(num).toBeGreaterThan(5);
expect(num).toBeCloseTo(0.3, 5);
expect(fn).toThrow("error message");
await expect(asyncFn()).resolves.toBe(value);
await expect(asyncFn()).rejects.toThrow();

// Snapshots
expect(data).toMatchSnapshot();
expect(data).toMatchInlineSnapshot(`{ "key": "value" }`);
```

## bun install - Package Manager

Node.js-compatible package manager, up to 25x faster than npm with hardlinks, global cache, and workspace support.

```bash
# Install all dependencies
bun install

# Add packages
bun add react react-dom
bun add -d typescript @types/react  # dev dependency
bun add -o sharp                    # optional dependency

# Install specific versions
bun add react@18.2.0
bun add react@latest

# Global packages
bun add -g typescript

# Remove packages
bun remove lodash

# Update packages
bun update
bun update react

# Check outdated
bun outdated

# Production install (no devDependencies)
bun install --production

# CI mode - fail if lockfile out of sync
bun ci  # or bun install --frozen-lockfile

# Generate yarn.lock for compatibility
bun install --yarn
```

```json
// package.json - workspace configuration
{
  "name": "my-monorepo",
  "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"],
  "trustedDependencies": ["esbuild", "sharp"],
  "overrides": {
    "lodash": "4.17.21"
  }
}
```

```toml
# bunfig.toml - configuration
[install]
optional = true
dev = true
peer = true
frozenLockfile = false

[install.scopes]
"@mycompany" = "https://npm.mycompany.com"
```

## Bun.spawn - Child Processes

Spawn and manage child processes with streaming I/O and IPC support.

```typescript
// Simple execution
const proc = Bun.spawn(["echo", "Hello World"]);
await proc.exited;  // wait for completion
console.log(proc.exitCode);

// Capture output
const proc = Bun.spawn(["ls", "-la"], {
  cwd: "/tmp",
  env: { ...process.env, FOO: "bar" },
  stdout: "pipe",
  stderr: "pipe",
});

const output = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
console.log(output);

// Stream stdin
const proc = Bun.spawn(["cat"], {
  stdin: "pipe",
  stdout: "pipe",
});
proc.stdin.write("Hello ");
proc.stdin.write("World");
proc.stdin.end();
const result = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();

// Synchronous execution
const result = Bun.spawnSync(["echo", "Hello"]);
console.log(result.stdout.toString());  // "Hello\n"
console.log(result.exitCode);           // 0

// IPC communication
const child = Bun.spawn(["bun", "child.ts"], {
  ipc(message) {
    console.log("From child:", message);
  },
});
child.send({ type: "ping" });

// In child.ts:
process.on("message", (msg) => {
  if (msg.type === "ping") {
    process.send({ type: "pong" });
  }
});
```

## Bun.password - Password Hashing

Secure password hashing using Argon2 or bcrypt with automatic salt generation.

```typescript
// Hash password (Argon2id by default)
const hash = await Bun.password.hash("my-password");
// $argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=2,p=1$...

// Verify password
const isValid = await Bun.password.verify("my-password", hash);
console.log(isValid);  // true

// Use bcrypt
const bcryptHash = await Bun.password.hash("my-password", {
  algorithm: "bcrypt",
  cost: 12,  // work factor (default 10)
});

// Argon2 configuration
const argonHash = await Bun.password.hash("my-password", {
  algorithm: "argon2id",  // or "argon2i", "argon2d"
  memoryCost: 65536,      // KB
  timeCost: 3,            // iterations
});

// Sync versions
const hashSync = Bun.password.hashSync("password");
const validSync = Bun.password.verifySync("password", hashSync);
```

## Bun.Glob - Pattern Matching

Fast glob pattern matching for file system operations.

```typescript
const glob = new Bun.Glob("**/*.ts");

// Scan files matching pattern
for await (const file of glob.scan({ cwd: "./src" })) {
  console.log(file);  // relative paths
}

// Scan with options
for await (const file of glob.scan({
  cwd: "./src",
  absolute: true,      // return absolute paths
  onlyFiles: true,     // exclude directories
  followSymlinks: false,
})) {
  console.log(file);
}

// Test if string matches pattern
const glob = new Bun.Glob("*.json");
console.log(glob.match("package.json"));  // true
console.log(glob.match("src/data.json")); // false

// Supported patterns:
// *     - match any characters except /
// **    - match any characters including /
// ?     - match single character
// [abc] - match character class
// {a,b} - match alternatives
```

## Utilities

Various built-in utilities for common operations.

```typescript
// Sleep
await Bun.sleep(1000);  // milliseconds
await Bun.sleepSync(1000);

// Generate UUID
const uuid = crypto.randomUUID();

// Deep equality check
const isEqual = Bun.deepEquals({ a: 1 }, { a: 1 });  // true

// Escape HTML
const safe = Bun.escapeHTML("<script>alert('xss')</script>");
// &lt;script&gt;alert(&#x27;xss&#x27;)&lt;/script&gt;

// Gzip compression
const compressed = Bun.gzipSync(Buffer.from("Hello World"));
const decompressed = Bun.gunzipSync(compressed);

// Deflate compression
const deflated = Bun.deflateSync(Buffer.from("Hello World"));
const inflated = Bun.inflateSync(deflated);

// Get Bun version
console.log(Bun.version);    // "1.0.0"
console.log(Bun.revision);   // git commit hash

// Detect Bun runtime
if (typeof Bun !== "undefined") {
  console.log("Running in Bun");
}

// Check if file is main entry point
if (import.meta.main) {
  console.log("This is the entry point");
}

// File metadata
console.log(import.meta.file);  // filename
console.log(import.meta.dir);   // directory
console.log(import.meta.path);  // full path

// Resolve path
const resolved = import.meta.resolve("./module.ts");

// Which - find executable
const bunPath = Bun.which("bun");  // "/usr/local/bin/bun"
```

## Summary

Bun provides a complete JavaScript/TypeScript development toolkit with exceptional performance. The HTTP server (`Bun.serve`) handles routing, WebSockets, and static files in a single unified API. The SQLite driver (`bun:sqlite`) offers synchronous, high-performance database access with transactions and class mapping. The shell API (`$`) enables cross-platform scripting with JavaScript interop and automatic security escaping.

For building and testing, `Bun.build` bundles applications with tree-shaking and code splitting, while `bun:test` provides Jest-compatible testing with mocks, snapshots, and coverage. The package manager (`bun install`) dramatically speeds up dependency installation with hardlinks and a global cache. Together, these tools form a cohesive ecosystem for building modern JavaScript applications with minimal configuration and maximum performance.
